title: Hartogia cuspidata
intro: Hartogia cuspidata, also known as Hartogia, is a plant species from the family Rubiaceae, native to the tropical regions of Africa. The health benefits of Hartogia cuspidata include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Hartogia cuspidata include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Hartogia cuspidata is commonly used in traditional herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, as well as in the form of extracts and tinctures, to take advantage of its therapeutic benefits.
taxonomy: Hartogia cuspidata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is a genus of flowering plants that is endemic to Madagascar. The plant is part of the family Apocynaceae which includes a wide range of plants such as periwinkle and oleander. The classification of Hartogia cuspidata is within the kingdom Plantae, which includes all other plants. This plant is further classified as a member of the subfamily Rauvolfioideae, which includes various other species of flowering plants.
